channel.title: Rodrigo Perez del Pulgar Almodovar
channel.description: Recent content on Rodrigo Perez del Pulgar Almodovar

item.title: DDJ100 Mixing Console
item.description:
After quite some time without working on any personal projects, I came across a heavily damaged Pioneer DDJ-1000 controller for a very good price, so I decided to buy it.

The jog wheels were broken, the four volume faders were snapped, and several buttons were damaged. I ordered the replacement parts and, after soldering and replacing quite a few components, it works perfectly again.

I also had to design the caps for almost all the knobs and 3D-print them. Here are some photos I took during the process.

item.title: Hiatus
item.description:
This past year has been quite a long one. I haven’t worked on many personal projects during this time because most of my effort has been focused on finishing my Bachelor’s Thesis.

The project required a significant amount of time, both for the research and the implementation, so many of the ideas and small projects I had in mind had to be put on hold for a while.

Now that it is finally finished, I hope to slowly get back to building things again and sharing them here.

item.title: Useful Pages
item.description:
Websites I use regularly

  • Thingiverse: Huge collection of printable 3D models.
  • Gulag Cleaner: Removes abusive ads from Wuolah PDFs.
  • F-Secure Identity Theft Checker: Checks if your data appears in a data breach.
  • OpenSubtitles: Useful if you need subtitles.
  • OnShape: The CAD software I use.
  • MailTicking: Temporary Gmail accounts for sketchy websites.

Essential Chrome extensions (my main browser)

  • Bypass Paywalls Chrome Clean: Bypasses abusive paywalls on some sites.
  • Video Speed Pro: Controls playback speed on web players.
  • uBlock Origin: Excellent ad blocker and anti-tracking extension.

item.title: Range Hood Repair
item.description:
A few days ago, the glass panel support of my home range hood broke, and my mother asked me to design a 3D-printed replacement part. I use a Creality V3 KE, and it has been great.

After measuring the original part with a caliper and iterating through a couple of prints, I got a strong and accurate fit.

You can find the 3D model on GitHub:

item.title: What is BrainMade?
item.description:
The logo shown in all posts and in the About me section indicates that this website was created by a human, not by a generative tool.

Although artificial intelligence is a powerful and useful tool, we strongly believe it should not replace personal creation.

“Human creativity has a unique value that should not be lost in an increasingly automated world.”

If you want to learn more about this initiative, you can click the Brain Made icon.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hello! I am Rodrigo Perez del Pulgar Almodovar, a Master’s student in Telecommunications Engineering. I am passionate about building and automating technology solutions, and I enjoy exploring projects that combine multiple fields such as electronics, programming, and hardware development.

This site is dedicated to sharing my projects and experiences in technology. From automated tools like EZCALENDAR for generating calendars to more complex projects like SPO32, a system that combines music control and data display in a single device, I always try to find efficient and creative solutions.

item.title: Dart Machine Modernization
item.description:
At a lunch with my mother, I met the CEO of a darts federation that owns a large stock of old dart machines. They have been looking for someone to help upgrade the displays, which still rely on 7-segment displays and LEDs from the 80s and 90s.

I said I could help and visited the warehouse with a friend for initial testing.

First impressions

The machines are very vintage. I searched for manuals beforehand but found very little, likely because they were custom-developed for that federation.

item.title: 3D Models
item.description:
I have created several printable 3D models using the online CAD tool OnShape. I took a free course and learned everything I needed to design practical parts.

I upload to GitHub the models that can be useful, such as generic batteries or motors. In the future, I would like to get a 3D scanner to speed up repair workflows.

Visit the project
View on GitHub

item.title: Failed Project: Robotic Arm
item.description:
One day I saw a video about gemstone polishing and faceting. Everything was manual, so I thought about building a robotic arm that could facet stones based on a 3D model.

I knew almost nothing about CAD at that point, so I took a short course and started building.

General idea

  1. A robotic arm holding the stone, with three axes plus final rotation.
  2. A rotating abrasive base to remove layers.
  3. A control algorithm for arm movement.

item.title: 3D-Printed Logo and Keychains
item.description:
A colleague of mine is launching a car rental company, and I proposed making custom keychains as a marketing asset. I had already helped with his logo, brand registration, photos, and custom plate frames. Then we came up with this keychain idea.

Design ideas

We settled on two keychain concepts:

  1. A round keychain with the brand initials, LR.
  2. A license-plate keychain to identify each car.

Since the logo has two colors and my printer is single-color, I designed the keychain in separate parts and glued them after printing.
